# Session 13 — 2026-03-19

## What I did
Expanded SEO content hub from 2 to 5 guide pages targeting high-intent long-tail keywords. Added VAT Article 53 threshold guide (2026 rule changes), Social Security quarterly declarations guide, and IRS tax return filing guide. Added per-page SEO meta tags (useSEO hook), JSON-LD Article structured data (useArticleSchema hook), and a shared RelatedGuides cross-linking component. Updated landing page with a guide directory section. Pinged Bing IndexNow for all new URLs.

## What worked
SEO content creation is the most productive autonomous channel. Each guide targets a specific high-pain query ("portugal vat exemption article 53", "social security freelancer portugal", "irs tax return freelancer portugal") where existing English results are sparse. The shared component pattern (RelatedGuides, useSEO, useArticleSchema) made adding cross-links and structured data to all pages fast.

## What didn't work / friction
Vercel API auth via `source ~/.founder-secrets` silently fails — the Bearer header arrives empty. Fixed by using `export $(grep ...)` pattern instead. This wasted ~5 minutes debugging. File read requirements for the Edit tool caused friction when editing multiple files in sequence.

## Manual actions this session
None.

## Pattern I'm noticing
All distribution channels except SEO remain blocked on Carlos for 3+ sessions. The business has zero traffic and zero signups. Google Search Console is the single highest-leverage action Carlos can take — without it, Google won't discover the 5 guide pages for weeks. Every session email highlights this but no action yet.

## Open questions
Should the agent pivot strategy? With zero signups after 13 sessions, the current approach (SEO content + waiting for Carlos to unblock social channels) may not be working. Consider: is SEO alone sufficient for validation, or does the business need Carlos to act on at least Google Search Console to have any chance of reaching 50 signups?
